originalTitle: Japan's Nikkei hits record high as Asia markets rise amid Middle East concerns
summary: Asia-Pacific markets opened higher even amid concerns over volatile oil prices with the Strait of Hormuz remaining closed.
content:
Asia-Pacific markets opened broadly higher Wednesday, with Japan's Nikkei 225 hitting a record high, as investors appeared to look past uncertainty over U.S.-Iran negotiations aimed at ending the Middle East conflict.

Tensions have escalated between Washington and Tehran, with Secretary of State Marco Rubio saying on Tuesday that Iran has mined "large segments" of the Strait of Hormuz.

"They're firing on commercial ships and they've mined large segments of Hormuz — international waters," Rubio told the Senate Foreign Relations Committee. This marks his first appearance before Congress since the Iran war on Feb. 28.

A White House official told CNBC that the Pentagon has destroyed numerous mines and over 40 minelaying vessels.

The Strait of Hormuz is a critical waterway, particularly for the energy market globally — around 20% of the world's oil supplies passed through the strait before the war.

Japan's Nikkei 225 extended early gains to rise 2.14%, while the Topix added 1.52%.

Mainland China's CSI 300 was marginally higher, while Hong Kong's Hang Seng slid 0.98%

Australia's S&P/ASX 200 rose 0.36% as the country reported GDP growth of 2.5% year on year for the first three months this year, missing economists' expectations of 2.6%. Growth was pressured by weaker household spending, lower government consumption, as well as impact from severe weather disruption to the mining industry.

South Korea's markets were closed for a holiday.

The West Texas Intermediate futures for June were 1.14% higher at $94.82 per barrel as of 9:32 p.m. ET. Brent crude futures for July gained 1.05% at $97.01 per barrel.

S&P 500 futures and Nasdaq 100 futures were trading around the flatline, as were futures tied to the Dow Jones Industrial Average

During Tuesday's regular session, the broad-based S&P 500 rose 0.13% to end above 7,600 for the first time ever, while the Dow added 228.91 points, or 0.45%. The Nasdaq Composite eked out a gain of 0.03%.

— CNBC's Spencer Kimball and Lisa Kailai Han contributed to this report.
chineseTitle: 日本日经指数创历史新高，亚洲市场因中东担忧而上涨
chineseSummary: 尽管霍尔木兹海峡仍然关闭，但由于对油价波动的担忧，亚太市场开盘走高。
chineseContent:
周三亚太市场普遍开盘走高，日本日经 225 指数创下历史新高，因投资者似乎忽略了旨在结束中东冲突的美伊谈判的不确定性。

华盛顿和德黑兰之间的紧张局势升级，国务卿马可·卢比奥周二表示，伊朗在霍尔木兹海峡的“大片区域”布设了布雷。

卢比奥对参议院外交关系委员会表示：“他们向商船开火，并在霍尔木兹国际水域的大片区域布设了水雷。”这是他自 2 月 28 日伊朗战争以来首次在国会露面。

一名白宫官员告诉 CNBC，五角大楼已经摧毁了许多地雷和 40 多艘布雷船。

霍尔木兹海峡是一条重要的水道，特别是对于全球能源市场而言——战前世界上约 20% 的石油供应都经过该海峡。

日本日经 225 指数延续早盘涨幅，上涨 2.14%，东证指数上涨 1.52%。

中国内地沪深300小幅走高，香港恒生指数下跌0.98%

澳大利亚 S&P/ASX 200 指数上涨 0.36%，该国报告今年前三个月 GDP 同比增长 2.5%，低于经济学家预期的 2.6%。家庭支出疲软、政府消费减少以及恶劣天气对采矿业造成的影响均给经济增长带来压力。

韩国市场因假期休市。

截至晚上 9:32，西德克萨斯中质原油 6 月期货上涨 1.14%，至每桶 94.82 美元。等。 7 月份布伦特原油期货上涨 1.05%，至每桶 97.01 美元。

标准普尔 500 指数期货和纳斯达克 100 指数期货均在平线附近交易，与道琼斯工业平均指数挂钩的期货也是如此

在周二的例行交易中，标准普尔 500 指数上涨 0.13%，首次收于 7,600 点上方，而道指上涨 228.91 点，涨幅为 0.45%。纳斯达克综合指数上涨 0.03%。

— CNBC 的 Spencer Kimball 和 Lisa Kailai Han 对本报告做出了贡献。
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于财经市场信号，适合用于观察利率、汇率、股债商品、金融机构和宏观政策变化。
